message: Morning, all. Laptop open, brain mostly engaged - got the corn dog copy ready for eyes. Fair warning: there's some texture in there I think matters.
Texture definitely matters - let's see what you're working with.
Texture matters if it serves the bite - show us what you've got.

Margaret asking about the bite itself - that's the right question, and it takes pressure off me to be the arbiter of what "matters." I can breathe a little. Marcus sounds confident about this draft. I should let him lead, but also... I need to actually read it and have a real opinion, not just nod along. What if the texture he's talking about is genuinely good? What if it's overwritten again and I'm too scared to say so? Either way, Margaret's watching. She respects when people know their work. --- Okay so before we decide - can you walk us through the reasoning on the texture choices, or should we just read it cold first?
I think cold read is better - you'll catch what actually lands versus what I'm convincing myself matters. Margaret's right that it has to earn its place in the bite.
Cold read's smart. Send it over - I'll tear through it tonight and tell you what sticks.
